What entities are present and what are they doing during the lifter preparation stage?
canonical_answer: A person wearing a dark long-sleeved shirt opens a black and white box labeled COMP Cams. Silver metal lifters are placed into a dark plastic tray containing brown liquid. A white text box overlay instructs to soak new lifters in 10W30 synthetic oil. The workbench surface is dark and appears worn with metal shavings visible nearby.

question: Describe the sequence of visual events shown from the introduction to the fluid draining step.
canonical_answer: At 0s, a cartoon truck graphic with a cat driver appears; at 6s, a title card reads HOW TO Swap a CAMSHAFT; at 8s, hands pour fluid into a glass dish near a COMP Cams box; at 16s, dark liquid drains from a red container into a bucket.

question: Walk through the specific maintenance actions and engine views presented during the preparation and draining phases.
canonical_answer: At 8s, text instructs to soak new lifters in synthetic oil; at 10s, hands place silver metal lifters into a dark plastic tray; at 16s, a hand holds a drain plug over a bucket; at 18s, a red engine block with black valve covers is shown.
